ArchitectureNotes.objective: Package portable OpenCode workflow plugins while keeping vvoc-owned config and memory data isolated from native OpenCode config, and replace target-based model overrides with a semantic role system that resolves `vv-role:*` references at runtime.
ArchitectureNotes.non-goal: Do not preserve backward compatibility with pre-role vvoc schemas or continue exposing target-based model override UX once the role-based contract is introduced.
ArchitectureNotes.risk-1: Config file mutation must stay conservative because users may already have hand-maintained JSONC with comments.
ArchitectureNotes.risk-2: The file-based memory store does full scans and should stay explicit, simple, and easy to reason about until scale requires indexing.
ArchitectureNotes.risk-3: Shared workflows may reference custom roles that are not assigned on another machine, so runtime role resolution must fail loudly and point at the offending config field.
ArchitectureNotes.risk-4: Provider-level alias models such as `openai/vv-gpt-5.4-xhigh` remain a separate lower-level mechanism, so the role system must compose with provider patch commands without silently requiring them.

contract.purpose: Define built-in role IDs, `vv-role:*` reference syntax, concrete model-selection parsing, canonical role assignment normalization, shared role resolution helpers, and built-in agent-to-role mappings.

export-isRoleReference.PURPOSE: Check whether a model string uses the `vv-role:*` reference syntax.

export-parseModelSelection.PURPOSE: Parse `provider/model[:variant]` selections used by canonical role assignments.

export-resolveRoleReference.PURPOSE: Resolve a `vv-role:*` reference against the canonical role map and return the concrete model selection.

export-getBuiltInRoleBindings.PURPOSE: Return the hard-coded built-in role mapping used by managed agents and OpenCode defaults.

notes.note-2: Custom roles are allowed, but roles do not resolve transitively to other roles in v1 of this redesign.

contract.purpose: Resolve OpenCode and canonical vvoc config paths, edit JSONC safely, pin the current vvoc package version in OpenCode config, write role-reference based OpenCode defaults and managed agents conservatively, render canonical vvoc schema v3, refresh built-in role presets, and inspect installation state.

export-resolvePaths.PURPOSE: Resolve OpenCode config paths plus the canonical vvoc.json path for the selected scope.

export-ensurePackageInstalled.PURPOSE: Ensure the npm package is registered in OpenCode config with a pinned package version.

export-syncManagedAgentRegistrations.PURPOSE: Ensure OpenCode config contains the managed enhancer and bundled subagent registrations wired to `vv-role:*` references.

export-syncVvocConfig.PURPOSE: Rewrite the canonical vvoc.json document as schema v3 while preserving valid current role-based values and refreshing managed `vv-*` role presets.

notes.note-1: Main OpenCode config edits should preserve comments when only the plugin array, role references, or managed agent registrations change.
notes.note-2: Pre-role vvoc schemas are rejected instead of migrated.

contract.purpose: Expose init, install, sync, status, doctor, version, role, preset, plugin list, config validate, patch-provider, completion, guardian config, and upgrade subcommands through citty while delegating role-aware config mutation to the helper layer.

export-init.PURPOSE: Bootstrap package registration and role-based config scaffolding (interactive by default).

export-sync.PURPOSE: Refresh canonical vvoc.json, role presets, managed prompts, and role-reference OpenCode config.

export-doctor.PURPOSE: Diagnose installation drift, unsupported vvoc schema versions, invalid role assignments, and unresolved role references.

notes.note-1: The `vvoc agent` command tree is removed as part of the hard cut to role-based model semantics.

contract.purpose: Resolve `vv-role:*` references inside OpenCode root model fields plus agent and command definitions during plugin config hooks so shared workflows bind semantic model intent to user-local provider choices at startup time.

param.name: Hooks
param.type: OpenCode plugin hooks with config mutation

export-ModelRolesPlugin.PURPOSE: Register a config hook that resolves `vv-role:*` references in supported OpenCode config fields.

notes.note-1: The plugin resolves `model`, `small_model`, `agent.*.model`, and `command.*.model` when they use `vv-role:*` syntax.

contract.purpose: Review OpenCode permission requests with a constrained Guardian agent whose runtime model resolves locally from the built-in `fast` role, while keeping safe fallback behavior and explicit threshold control.

export-GuardianPlugin.PURPOSE: Register Guardian hooks, config agent, built-in-role model selection, and permission review flow.

notes.note-1: Guardian no longer owns model fields in canonical vvoc config; only its non-model runtime policy remains there.

contract.purpose: Persist explicit memory entries in the vvoc XDG data root, keep shared scope global across projects, support scope-aware CRUD, and rank lexical search results while loading only non-model memory settings from canonical vvoc config.

export-loadMemoryRuntimeConfig.PURPOSE: Load vvoc memory settings from canonical global vvoc.json.

export-putMemory.PURPOSE: Create a scoped memory entry in the vvoc XDG data root.

export-searchMemories.PURPOSE: Return ranked lexical matches across relevant memory scopes.

notes.note-1: Memory remains explicit-only. The store must not inject entries into prompts or system context.

contract.purpose: Add explicit memory tools to OpenCode, register a report-only `memory-reviewer` subagent whose runtime model resolves from the built-in `fast` role, and inject a short system reminder that encourages proactive memory tool usage without loading stored entries.

param.name: Hooks
param.type: OpenCode plugin hooks with custom tools and config mutation

export-MemoryPlugin.PURPOSE: Register explicit memory tools and the `memory-reviewer` subagent with role-driven model selection.

notes.note-1: The `memory` section no longer owns reviewer model fields; reviewer selection comes from the built-in role system.

contract.purpose: Inject reusable vvoc system guidance into primary chat sessions while skipping known subagents, using agent-aware message hooks instead of the global system transform hook.

param.name: Hooks
param.type: OpenCode plugin hooks with config-aware chat.message mutation

export-SystemContextInjectionPlugin.PURPOSE: Inject main-session guidance such as proactive explore usage into `message.system` for eligible primary agents.

contract.purpose: Redact secrets from provider-bound payloads and restore placeholders after completions and before local tool execution.

export-SecretsRedactionPlugin.PURPOSE: Register secrets-redaction hooks for chat, completions, and tool execution.

contract.purpose: List, set, and unset semantic role assignments in canonical vvoc.json for both built-in and user-defined roles.

param.name: stdout
param.type: terminal output with role inventory or write summary

export-listRoles.PURPOSE: Render built-in and custom role assignments from canonical vvoc.json.

export-setRole.PURPOSE: Write a normalized concrete model selection for the selected role.

export-unsetRole.PURPOSE: Remove a custom role assignment without allowing built-in role deletion.

contract.purpose: List, show, and apply declarative named role presets stored in canonical vvoc.json without mutating OpenCode config directly.

export-resolvePreset.PURPOSE: Validate a preset name against the current canonical vvoc.json preset map.

export-applyPreset.PURPOSE: Apply only the listed role assignments through canonical vvoc config writes.

notes.note-1: Built-in presets remain `vv-openai`, `vv-zai`, and `vv-minimax` but now assign roles instead of target overrides.

contract.purpose: Validate canonical vvoc.json against the role-based vvoc JSON Schema v3 with human-readable error reporting and no compatibility mode for older schemas.

param.name: stdout
param.type: terminal output with pass/fail status for vvoc.json

export-validateVvocConfig.PURPOSE: Parse vvoc.json and report schema or semantic violations for roles and presets.

contract.purpose: Display all installed OpenCode plugins with their status (enabled/disabled) and source paths.

export-listPlugins.PURPOSE: Scan OpenCode config and display plugin list with status.

contract.purpose: Apply global OpenCode patch presets that rewrite provider.options.baseURL or merge provider-specific alias-model config under `provider` conservatively, without overwriting root OpenCode model fields that are now role-reference based.

param.name: WriteResult
param.type: created | updated | kept for global opencode.json/jsonc

export-resolvePatchProviderPreset.PURPOSE: Map a supported preset like stepfun-ai, zai, or openai to concrete OpenCode provider-object writes.

export-applyPatchProviderPreset.PURPOSE: Resolve global OpenCode config paths and apply the preset without mutating role-reference root defaults.

contract.purpose: Auto-detect the current shell and install vvoc completion files idempotently for bash, zsh, and fish, including patch-provider presets, role commands, role IDs, and preset names.

export-default.PURPOSE: Install shell completions for the detected shell.

export-detectShell.PURPOSE: Detect the active shell from environment variables.

contract.purpose: Interactive project initialization that registers @osovv/vv-opencode in the OpenCode plugin array, scaffolds managed prompts, seeds canonical role assignments, and writes role-reference based OpenCode config defaults. Uses @clack/prompts for TTY prompts.
Check npm for a newer vvoc release, install it globally with Bun, and trigger the default global sync flow from a fresh subprocess.

param.name: stdout
param.type: upgrade status, optional changelog, install progress, and sync results

export-runUpgradeFlow.PURPOSE: Execute the full global-only upgrade flow and warn instead of failing when post-install sync cannot complete.

export-fetchLatestVersion.PURPOSE: Query npm for the latest @osovv/vv-opencode version.

export-fetchChangelog.PURPOSE: Fetch and display best-effort release notes for the latest version.

DF-PLUGIN-INSTALL.NAME: PluginInstall
DF-PLUGIN-INSTALL.step-1: Resolve scope-specific OpenCode config paths plus the canonical global vvoc.json path.
DF-PLUGIN-INSTALL.step-2: Ensure `@osovv/vv-opencode@<installed-version>` exists in the OpenCode `plugin` array.
DF-PLUGIN-INSTALL.step-3: Sync managed agent registrations so OpenCode defaults and managed agents point at `vv-role:*` references instead of concrete provider/model strings.
DF-PLUGIN-INSTALL.step-4: Create a fully seeded canonical vvoc schema v3 document with built-in roles and managed `vv-*` role presets.
DF-PLUGIN-INSTALL.evidence: `vvoc status`, parsed config content, and role-reference OpenCode config after install.

DF-CONFIG-SYNC.NAME: ManagedConfigSync
DF-CONFIG-SYNC.step-1: Inspect canonical vvoc.json and reject unsupported pre-role schema versions.
DF-CONFIG-SYNC.step-3: Rewrite vvoc.json canonically while preserving valid role assignments and refreshing managed `vv-*` role presets.
DF-CONFIG-SYNC.step-4: Rewrite OpenCode defaults and managed agents back to canonical `vv-role:*` references if drift occurred.
DF-CONFIG-SYNC.evidence: Stable rewritten vvoc.json, scope-correct managed prompt sync, and role-reference OpenCode config.

DF-ROLE-MANAGEMENT.NAME: RoleManagement
DF-ROLE-MANAGEMENT.step-1: Load or create the canonical vvoc schema v3 document.
DF-ROLE-MANAGEMENT.step-2: Validate the requested role ID and normalize any concrete `provider/model[:variant]` assignment.
DF-ROLE-MANAGEMENT.step-3: Write the updated role map or render the current role inventory.
DF-ROLE-MANAGEMENT.evidence: Built-in roles are always present, valid custom roles round-trip cleanly, and builtin role deletion is blocked.

DF-ROLE-RESOLUTION.NAME: RoleResolution
DF-ROLE-RESOLUTION.step-1: Load the canonical role map from global vvoc.json.
DF-ROLE-RESOLUTION.step-2: Walk supported OpenCode model-bearing fields and detect `vv-role:*` references.
DF-ROLE-RESOLUTION.step-3: Resolve each role reference to a concrete model selection, splitting `provider/model:variant` into native `model` plus `variant` fields where the target supports variants.
DF-ROLE-RESOLUTION.step-4: Throw an explicit error for unknown roles or unsupported concrete assignments instead of silently falling back.
DF-ROLE-RESOLUTION.evidence: Agent, command, and root model fields materialize user-local model choices from semantic role references.

DF-PRESET-SWITCH.NAME: PresetSwitch
DF-PRESET-SWITCH.step-1: Read configured presets from canonical vvoc.json and validate the requested preset name.
DF-PRESET-SWITCH.step-2: Normalize the preset's concrete role assignments.
DF-PRESET-SWITCH.step-3: Apply only the listed role assignments to canonical vvoc.json.
DF-PRESET-SWITCH.step-4: Leave OpenCode config untouched because it already points at stable `vv-role:*` references.
DF-PRESET-SWITCH.evidence: Selected roles change, unlisted roles remain untouched, and CLI output prints a per-role summary.

DF-GUARDIAN-REVIEW.NAME: GuardianPermissionReview
DF-GUARDIAN-REVIEW.step-0: Resolve the built-in `fast` role to the concrete Guardian runtime model before registering the hidden subagent.
DF-GUARDIAN-REVIEW.step-1: Capture relevant tool and command intent for the session.
DF-GUARDIAN-REVIEW.step-2: Build a bounded transcript and planned action prompt.
DF-GUARDIAN-REVIEW.step-3: Run Guardian, interpret risk, and auto-reply only when below threshold.
DF-GUARDIAN-REVIEW.evidence: Guardian logs, toasts, and permission reply behavior inside OpenCode.

DF-MEMORY-ACCESS.NAME: MemoryAccess
DF-MEMORY-ACCESS.step-0: Before model turns, the plugin appends a short system reminder that explicit memory tools exist, without injecting stored memory entries.
DF-MEMORY-ACCESS.step-2: Resolve relevant scopes from session id, git branch, project, or shared key.
DF-MEMORY-ACCESS.step-3: Read or mutate session, branch, and project entries under `$XDG_DATA_HOME/vvoc/projects/<project-id>/memory/`, and shared entries under `$XDG_DATA_HOME/vvoc/memory/shared/<namespace>/`.
DF-MEMORY-ACCESS.evidence: Correct file layout, deterministic CRUD/search behavior, and no automatic prompt injection.

DF-MEMORY-REVIEW.NAME: MemoryReview
DF-MEMORY-REVIEW.step-1: Resolve the built-in `fast` role and register the reviewer subagent through plugin config mutation.
DF-MEMORY-REVIEW.step-2: The reviewer inspects memory with `memory_list`, `memory_get`, and `memory_search` only.
DF-MEMORY-REVIEW.step-3: The reviewer returns a keep/update/merge/delete report without modifying entries.
DF-MEMORY-REVIEW.evidence: Bundled `memory-reviewer` agent with read-only memory tooling and built-in role-driven model selection.

DF-SYSTEM-CONTEXT.NAME: SystemContextInjection
DF-SYSTEM-CONTEXT.step-1: Build the known-subagent set from built-ins, plugin-managed agents, and config-defined subagents.
DF-SYSTEM-CONTEXT.step-2: Inspect the resolved agent name on each outgoing chat message.
DF-SYSTEM-CONTEXT.step-3: Append reusable vvoc system guidance only for eligible primary agents.
DF-SYSTEM-CONTEXT.step-4: Skip known subagents and internal helper agents so their prompts stay isolated.
DF-SYSTEM-CONTEXT.evidence: Primary agents receive one reusable guidance block; known subagents do not inherit it.

DF-SECRETS-REDACTION.NAME: SecretsRedaction
DF-SECRETS-REDACTION.TRIGGER: provider-bound request or tool execution with secrets present
DF-SECRETS-REDACTION.step-1: Load the secrets-redaction section from canonical vvoc.json with environment-variable substitution.
DF-SECRETS-REDACTION.step-2: Replace detected secrets in model-bound chat payloads with stable session placeholders.
DF-SECRETS-REDACTION.step-3: Restore placeholders in model completions and in tool arguments before local execution.
DF-SECRETS-REDACTION.step-4: Preserve session-local placeholder mappings with TTL and bounded cache behavior.
DF-SECRETS-REDACTION.evidence: Provider-bound payloads are redacted, while local tool execution and user-visible responses receive restored values where needed.

DF-PLUGIN-LIST.NAME: PluginList
DF-PLUGIN-LIST.step-1: Resolve OpenCode config path for the current scope.
DF-PLUGIN-LIST.step-2: Parse plugin array and extract each plugin's source.
DF-PLUGIN-LIST.step-3: Display plugin table with name, source, and enabled status.
DF-PLUGIN-LIST.evidence: Correct plugin table when plugins are installed, and graceful empty state when none are configured.

DF-CONFIG-VALIDATE.NAME: ConfigValidate
DF-CONFIG-VALIDATE.step-2: Parse the file with strict JSON rules.
DF-CONFIG-VALIDATE.step-3: Validate required fields, built-in role presence, and preset role assignments against the versioned vvoc JSON Schema v3.
DF-CONFIG-VALIDATE.step-4: Reject unsupported vvoc schema versions instead of migrating them.
DF-CONFIG-VALIDATE.evidence: Invalid JSON is caught and reported with line/column info; valid canonical role-based configs pass; older schemas fail loudly.

DF-COMPLETION.NAME: ShellCompletion
DF-COMPLETION.step-1: Detect the current shell from environment variables.
DF-COMPLETION.step-2: Generate shell-specific completion content for bash, zsh, or fish, including `role` subcommands and built-in role IDs.
DF-COMPLETION.step-3: Write the completion file to the shell-specific location.
DF-COMPLETION.step-4: Patch shell init files idempotently when the shell needs an explicit loader.
DF-COMPLETION.evidence: Installed completion files match the real vvoc command tree and role-centric subcommand surface.

DF-INIT.NAME: InteractiveInit
DF-INIT.step-1: Check --non-interactive flag. If absent and TTY detected, enter interactive mode with @clack/prompts.
DF-INIT.step-2: Prompt for scope selection (global/project) if interactive.
DF-INIT.step-3: Write initial managed prompts, managed OpenCode registrations, and canonical built-in role assignments.
DF-INIT.step-4: Ensure OpenCode defaults and managed agents point at canonical `vv-role:*` references.
DF-INIT.evidence: Canonical config is valid JSON after init; Ctrl+C aborts cleanly; re-running init is idempotent; non-interactive mode works without TTY.

DF-UPGRADE.NAME: UpgradeCheck
DF-UPGRADE.step-1: Query npm registry for @osovv/vv-opencode dist-tags latest.
DF-UPGRADE.step-3: If newer, fetch best-effort changelog context and install `@osovv/vv-opencode@<latest>` globally with `bun add -g`.
DF-UPGRADE.step-4: After a successful install, launch `vvoc sync` in a fresh subprocess so the new package version and sync logic are used.
DF-UPGRADE.step-5: If sync cannot run or exits non-zero, keep the upgrade successful but warn the user to run `vvoc sync` manually.
DF-UPGRADE.evidence: Network failure is handled gracefully; already-latest case prints OK; install failures stop before sync; successful installs trigger the default global sync flow.

Phase-1.name: CLI Foundation
Phase-1.goal: Establish safe config mutation helpers and wire them into a usable Bun CLI.
Phase-1.step-1: Implement path resolution, JSONC OpenCode edits, canonical vvoc.json helpers, and installation inspection.
Phase-1.step-2: Expose install, sync, status, doctor, and Guardian config commands.

Phase-2.name: Guardian Packaging
Phase-2.goal: Move the existing standalone Guardian plugin into the repository and export it from the package.
Phase-2.step-1: Port Guardian and move runtime config to the canonical vvoc.json path.

Phase-3.name: Explicit Memory
Phase-3.goal: Add explicit persistent memory, store it in the vvoc XDG data root, and expose a report-only reviewer subagent.
Phase-3.step-1: Implement file-based scoped memory storage and lexical search.
Phase-3.step-2: Register memory tools and the `memory-reviewer` subagent via OpenCode plugin hooks.

Phase-4.name: CLI Enhancement Suite
Phase-4.goal: Add preset/configuration helpers and developer-experience commands to improve setup, inspection, and switching flows.
Phase-4.step-1: Validate canonical vvoc.json with human-readable schema errors.
Phase-4.step-2: List installed plugins with status, enabled/disabled, and source path.
Phase-4.step-3: Apply supported OpenCode patch presets conservatively to the global OpenCode config.
Phase-4.step-4: List, inspect, and apply named presets from canonical vvoc.json.
Phase-4.step-5: Install shell completions for bash, zsh, and fish with shell auto-detection.
Phase-4.step-6: Interactive project setup with prompts for config location and plugin selection.
Phase-4.step-7: Check npm for updates, install the global package with Bun, and run post-install sync from a fresh subprocess.

Phase-5.name: Session Guidance And Safety
Phase-5.goal: Inject reusable main-session guidance and keep provider-bound payloads free of raw secrets.
Phase-5.step-1: Append reusable vvoc system guidance only for eligible primary agents via `chat.message`.
Phase-5.step-2: Redact secrets before LLM requests and restore placeholders after completion and before tool execution.

Phase-6.name: Role-Based Model Semantics
Phase-6.goal: Replace target-based model overrides with semantic role assignment, runtime `vv-role:*` resolution, and role-centric CLI workflows.
Phase-6.step-1: Implement the shared role contract, built-in role bindings, and concrete model-selection parsing helpers.
Phase-6.step-2: Upgrade canonical vvoc config to schema v3, seed built-in roles, remove target-based config fields, and keep OpenCode defaults and managed agents pinned to `vv-role:*` references.
Phase-6.step-3: Resolve role references at startup for supported OpenCode config fields and fail loudly for unknown roles.
Phase-6.step-4: Move Guardian model selection to the built-in role system without depending on plugin hook ordering.
Phase-6.step-5: Move memory-reviewer model selection to the built-in role system and drop reviewer model fields from canonical memory config.
Phase-6.step-6: Introduce the role command tree and remove the legacy `agent` override UX.
Phase-6.step-7: Convert built-in and user presets from target-based writes to role assignment writes only.
Phase-6.step-8: Keep provider alias patching as a lower-level helper for concrete role assignments without mutating root OpenCode model fields.
Phase-6.step-9: Update completion output for the new role command surface and built-in role IDs.
Phase-6.step-10: Seed role-based defaults during install/init and surface role state in status/doctor output.

ExecutionPolicy.worker-owns: Only package source files, tests, and narrowly scoped documentation updates required by the approved module changes.
